title: One client-agnostic MCP server, per-assistant config+grounding layers
context: Hunch MCP server works with any MCP-capable assistant, but each tool (Cursor, VS Code, Codex, etc.) has its own config format and grounding convention. Users need seamless multi-tool support without duplicating the `.hunch/` graph.
decision: Keep the MCP server client-agnostic; scaffold per-assistant integration layers (config files + grounding docs) that merge idempotently into existing user files. All tools point to the same `.hunch/` graph and share decision memory.
consequences: All assistants consult the same `.hunch/` graph and decision history
Single `hunch init` run configures Cursor, VS Code, Codex, and AGENTS.md automatically
Config writers handle merge logic and idempotency separately per tool (JSON for Cursor/VS Code, TOML for Codex, markdown for grounding)
Grounding content is sourced from one `renderHunchSection()` but written to multiple files (CLAUDE.md, .cursor/rules/hunch.mdc, .github/copilot-instructions.md, AGENTS.md)
Re-running `hunch init` is safe; no duplicates or conflicts
alternatives_rejected: Per-tool MCP servers — would fragment the graph and defeat shared memory goal
Manual per-tool configuration — error-prone, poor UX
Claude Code only — severely limits adoption and usefulness
Single centralized config format — violates each tool's native conventions and user expectations
